display.text:
import { HonoBase } from './hono-base'
import type { HonoOptions } from './hono-base'
import { RegExpRouter } from './router/reg-exp-router'
import { SmartRouter } from './router/smart-router'
import { TrieRouter } from './router/trie-router'
import type { BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema } from './types'

/**
 * The Hono class extends the functionality of the HonoBase class.
 * It sets up routing and allows for custom options to be passed.
 *
 * @template E - The environment type.
 * @template S - The schema type.
 * @template BasePath - The base path type.
 */
export class Hono<
  E extends Env = BlankEnv,
  S extends Schema = BlankSchema,
  BasePath extends string = '/',
> extends HonoBase<E, S, BasePath> {
  /**
   * Creates an instance of the Hono class.
   *
   * @param options - Optional configuration options for the Hono instance.
   */
  constructor(options: HonoOptions<E> = {}) {
    super(options)
    this.router =
      options.router ??
      new SmartRouter({
        routers: [new RegExpRouter(), new TrieRouter()],
      })
  }
}

/**
 * @module
 * This module provides types definitions and variables for the routers.
 */

/**
 * Constant representing all HTTP methods in uppercase.
 */
export const METHOD_NAME_ALL = 'ALL' as const
/**
 * Constant representing all HTTP methods in lowercase.
 */
export const METHOD_NAME_ALL_LOWERCASE = 'all' as const
/**
 * Array of supported HTTP methods.
 */
export const METHODS = ['get', 'post', 'put', 'delete', 'options', 'patch'] as const
/**
 * Error message indicating that a route cannot be added because the matcher is already built.
 */
export const MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT =
  'Can not add a route since the matcher is already built.'

/**
 * Interface representing a router.
 *
 * @template T - The type of the handler.
 */
export interface Router<T> {
  /**
   * The name of the router.
   */
  name: string

  /**
   * Adds a route to the router.
   *
   * @param method - The HTTP method (e.g., 'get', 'post').
   * @param path - The path for the route.
   * @param handler - The handler for the route.
   */
  add(method: string, path: string, handler: T): void

  /**
   * Matches a route based on the given method and path.
   *
   * @param method - The HTTP method (e.g., 'get', 'post').
   * @param path - The path to match.
   * @returns The result of the match.
   */
  match(method: string, path: string): Result<T>
}

/**
 * Type representing a map of parameter indices.
 */
export type ParamIndexMap = Record<string, number>
/**
 * Type representing a stash of parameters.
 */
export type ParamStash = string[]
/**
 * Type representing a map of parameters.
 */
export type Params = Record<string, string>
/**
 * Type representing the result of a route match.
 *
 * The result can be in one of two formats:
 * 1. An array of handlers with their corresponding parameter index maps, followed by a parameter stash.
 * 2. An array of handlers with their corresponding parameter maps.
 *
 * Example:
 *
 * [[handler, paramIndexMap][], paramArray]
 * ```typescript
 * [
 *   [
 *     [middlewareA, {}],                     // '*'
 *     [funcA,       {'id': 0}],              // '/user/:id/*'
 *     [funcB,       {'id': 0, 'action': 1}], // '/user/:id/:action'
 *   ],
 *   ['123', 'abc']
 * ]
 * ```
 *
 * [[handler, params][]]
 * ```typescript
 * [
 *   [
 *     [middlewareA, {}],                             // '*'
 *     [funcA,       {'id': '123'}],                  // '/user/:id/*'
 *     [funcB,       {'id': '123', 'action': 'abc'}], // '/user/:id/:action'
 *   ]
 * ]
 * ```
 */
export type Result<T> = [[T, ParamIndexMap][], ParamStash] | [[T, Params][]]

/**
 * Error class representing an unsupported path error.
 */
export class UnsupportedPathError extends Error {}

6: type HandlerSet<T> = {
7:   handler: T
8:   possibleKeys: string[]
9:   score: number
10: }
11: 
12: type HandlerParamsSet<T> = HandlerSet<T> & {
13:   params: Record<string, string>
14: }
15: 
16: const emptyParams = Object.create(null)
17: 
18: const hasChildren = (children: Record<string, unknown>): boolean => {
19:   for (const _ in children) {
20:     return true
21:   }
22:   return false
23: }
24: 
25: export class Node<T> {
26:   #methods: Record<string, HandlerSet<T>>[]
27: 
28:   #children: Record<string, Node<T>>
29:   #patterns: Pattern[]
30:   #order: number = 0
31:   #params: Record<string, string> = emptyParams
32: 
33:   constructor(method?: string, handler?: T, children?: Record<string, Node<T>>) {
34:     this.#children = children || Object.create(null)
35:     this.#methods = []
36:     if (method && handler) {
37:       const m: Record<string, HandlerSet<T>> = Object.create(null)
38:       m[method] = { handler, possibleKeys: [], score: 0 }
39:       this.#methods = [m]
40:     }
41:     this.#patterns = []
42:   }
43: 
44:   insert(method: string, path: string, handler: T): Node<T> {
45:     this.#order = ++this.#order
46: 
47:     // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-this-alias
48:     let curNode: Node<T> = this
49:     const parts = splitRoutingPath(path)
50: 
51:     const possibleKeys: string[] = []
52: 
53:     for (let i = 0, len = parts.length; i < len; i++) {
54:       const p: string = parts[i]
55:       const nextP = parts[i + 1]
56:       const pattern = getPattern(p, nextP)
57:       const key = Array.isArray(pattern) ? pattern[0] : p
58: 
59:       if (key in curNode.#children) {
60:         curNode = curNode.#children[key]
61:         if (pattern) {
62:           possibleKeys.push(pattern[1])
63:         }
64:         continue
65:       }
66: 
67:       curNode.#children[key] = new Node()
68: 
69:       if (pattern) {
70:         curNode.#patterns.push(pattern)
71:         possibleKeys.push(pattern[1])
72:       }
73:       curNode = curNode.#children[key]
74:     }
75: 
76:     curNode.#methods.push({
77:       [method]: {
78:         handler,
79:         possibleKeys: possibleKeys.filter((v, i, a) => a.indexOf(v) === i),
80:         score: this.#order,
81:       },
82:     })
83: 
84:     return curNode
85:   }
86: 
87:   #pushHandlerSets(
88:     handlerSets: HandlerParamsSet<T>[],
89:     node: Node<T>,
90:     method: string,
91:     nodeParams: Record<string, string>,
92:     params?: Record<string, string>
93:   ): void {

95:       const m = node.#methods[i]

97:       const processedSet: Record<number, boolean> = {}
98:       if (handlerSet !== undefined) {

100:         handlerSets.push(handlerSet)

112:   }

116:     this.#params = emptyParams

119:     const curNode: Node<T> = this
120:     let curNodes = [curNode]
121:     const parts = splitPath(path)
122:     const curNodesQueue: Node<T>[][] = []
123: 
124:     const len = parts.length
125:     let partOffsets: number[] | null = null
126: 
127:     for (let i = 0; i < len; i++) {
128:       const part: string = parts[i]
129:       const isLast = i === len - 1
130:       const tempNodes: Node<T>[] = []
131: 
132:       for (let j = 0, len2 = curNodes.length; j < len2; j++) {
133:         const node = curNodes[j]
134:         const nextNode = node.#children[part]
135: 
136:         if (nextNode) {
137:           nextNode.#params = node.#params
138:           if (isLast) {
139:             // '/hello/*' => match '/hello'
140:             if (nextNode.#children['*']) {
141:               this.#pushHandlerSets(handlerSets, nextNode.#children['*'], method, node.#params)
142:             }
143:             this.#pushHandlerSets(handlerSets, nextNode, method, node.#params)
144:           } else {
145:             tempNodes.push(nextNode)
146:           }
147:         }
148: 
149:         for (let k = 0, len3 = node.#patterns.length; k < len3; k++) {
150:           const pattern = node.#patterns[k]
151:           const params = node.#params === emptyParams ? {} : { ...node.#params }
152: 
153:           // Wildcard
154:           // '/hello/*/foo' => match /hello/bar/foo
155:           if (pattern === '*') {
156:             const astNode = node.#children['*']
157:             if (astNode) {
158:               this.#pushHandlerSets(handlerSets, astNode, method, node.#params)
159:               astNode.#params = params
160:               tempNodes.push(astNode)
161:             }
162:             continue
163:           }
164: 
165:           const [key, name, matcher] = pattern
166: 
167:           if (!part && !(matcher instanceof RegExp)) {
168:             continue
169:           }
170: 
171:           const child = node.#children[key]
172: 
173:           // `/js/:filename{[a-z]+.js}` => match /js/chunk/123.js

178:               for (let p = 0; p < len; p++) {

184: 
185:             const m = matcher.exec(restPathString)

188:               this.#pushHandlerSets(handlerSets, child, method, node.#params, params)
189:

193:                 const targetCurNodes = (curNodesQueue[componentCount] ||= [])
194:                 targetCurNodes.push(child)
195:               }
196: 
197:               continue
198:             }
199:           }
200: 
201:           if (matcher === true || matcher.test(part)) {
202:             params[name] = part
203:             if (isLast) {
204:               this.#pushHandlerSets(handlerSets, child, method, params, node.#params)
205:               if (child.#children['*']) {
206:                 this.#pushHandlerSets(

208:                   child.#children['*'],
209:                   method,

214:             } else {
215:               child.#params = params
216:               tempNodes.push(child)
217:             }
218:           }
219:         }
220:       }

222:       const shifted = curNodesQueue.shift()
223:       curNodes = shifted ? tempNodes.concat(shifted) : tempNodes
224:     }
225: 
226:     if (handlerSets.length > 1) {
227:       handlerSets.sort((a, b) => {
228:         return a.score - b.score
229:       })
230:     }
231: 
232:     return [handlerSets.map(({ handler, params }) => [handler, params] as [T, Params])]
233:   }
